retrieved_contexts.summary: To understand how ViT uses self-attention to integrate information across the image, we analyzed the average distance spanned by attention weights at different layers (Figure 11). This “attention distance” is analogous to receptive field size in CNNs. Average attention distance is highly variable across heads in lower layers, with some heads attending to much of the image, while others attend to small regions at or near the query location. As depth increases, attention distance increases for all heads. In the second half of the network, most heads attend widely across tokens.

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::48
retrieved_contexts.summary:
Self-attention allows ViT to integrate information across the entire image even in the lowest layers. We investigate to what degree the network makes use of this capability. Specifically, we compute the average distance in image space across which information is integrated, based on the attention weights (Figure 7, right). This “attention distance” is analogous to receptive field size in CNNs.

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::5
retrieved_contexts.summary:
Attention mechanisms have become an integral part of compelling sequence modeling and transduction models in various tasks, allowing modeling of dependencies without regard to their distance in the input or output sequences [2, 19]. In all but a few cases [27], however, such attention mechanisms are used in conjunction with a recurrent network.

In this work we propose the Transformer, a model architecture eschewing recurrence and instead relying entirely on an attention mechanism to draw global dependencies between input and output. The Transformer allows for significantly more parallelization and can reach a new state of the art in translation quality after being trained for as little as twelve hours on eight P100 GPUs.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::15
retrieved_contexts.summary: The two most commonly used attention functions are additive attention [2], and dot-product (multiplicative) attention. Dot-product attention is identical to our algorithm, except for the scaling factor of $\frac { 1 } { \sqrt { d _ { k } } }$ . Additive attention computes the compatibility function using a feed-forward network with a single hidden layer. While the two are similar in theoretical complexity, dot-product attention is much faster and more space-efficient in practice, since it can be implemented using highly optimized matrix multiplication code.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf

retrieved_contexts.summary: We find that some heads attend to most of the image already in the lowest layers, showing that the ability to integrate information globally is indeed used by the model. Other attention heads have consistently small attention distances in the low layers. This highly localized attention is less pronounced in hybrid models that apply a ResNet before the Transformer (Figure 7, right), suggesting that it may serve a similar function as early convolutional layers in CNNs. Further, the attention distance increases with network depth. Globally, we find that the model attends to image regions that are semantically relevant for classification (Figure 6).

Briefly, we averaged attention weights of ViT-L/16 across all heads and then recursively multiplied the weight matrices of all layers. This accounts for the mixing of attention across tokens through all layers.

question: 对比 Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) 和 ViT (Dosovitskiy et al., 2021) 中“学习率调度”的差异。Transformer 使用 \( lrate = d_{\text{model}}^{-0.5} \cdot \min(step\_num^{-0.5}, step\_num \cdot warmup\_steps^{-1.5}) \)（平方根倒数衰减），ViT 使用线性衰减（linear decay）。请分析两种调度策略的适用场景。
ground_truth: Transformer 的平方根倒数衰减在预热后缓慢下降，适合训练步数非常多的场景（如机器翻译的 100k-300k 步），因为下降速度逐渐减慢，允许模型在后期进行精细调优。ViT 的线性衰减在预热后线性下降，适合训练步数固定且相对较短（如 ViT 在 JFT 上训练 7-14 epoch）的场景，线性衰减简单且易于调整。两种策略都包含预热阶段（warmup），以稳定初始训练。
answer:
根据提供的检索证据，关于 Transformer 和 ViT 中“学习率调度”的具体公式差异及适用场景分析，**文献未提及**。

现有文档仅涉及训练设置的部分描述，并未包含您提到的具体调度公式或对比分析：
1.  文档 [7] 提到 ViT 微调时使用 SGD 并进行学习率网格搜索，但未明确说明具体的衰减策略（如线性衰减）[7]。
2.  文档 [2] 和 [5] 介绍了 Transformer 的背景及其在机器翻译中的应用，但未包含 Vaswani et al. (2017) 原文中的学习率调度数学公式 [2][5]。
3.  其他片段主要关注模型架构（如自注意力机制 [4]）、正则化类型 [10] 或特定任务性能 [1]，均未涉及学习率调度策略的适用场景讨论。

因此，无法基于当前知识库对两种调度策略的适用场景进行分析。

retrieved_contexts.summary: An overview of the model is depicted in Figure 1. The standard Transformer receives as input a 1D sequence of token embeddings. To handle 2D images, we reshape the image $\mathbf { x } \in \mathbb { R } ^ { H \times W \times \dot { C } }$ into a sequence of flattened 2D patches $\mathbf { x } _ { p } \in \mathbb { R } ^ { N \times ( P ^ { 2 } \cdot C ) }$ , where $( H , W )$ is the resolution of the original image, $C$ is the number of channels, $( P , P )$ is the resolution of each image patch, and $N = H W / P ^ { 2 }$ is the resulting number of patches, which also serves as the effective input sequence length for the Transformer. The Transformer uses constant latent vector size $D$ through all of its layers, so we flatten the patches and map to $D$ dimensions with a trainable linear projection (Eq. 1). We refer to the output of this projection as the patch embeddings.

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::2
retrieved_contexts.summary: The Transformer [49] is well acknowledged as the most powerful neural network in modelling sequential data, such as natural language [11, 35, 6] and speech [17]. Model variants built upon Transformer have also been shown great performance in computer vision [12, 36] and programming language [19, 63, 44]. However, to the best of our knowledge, Transformer has still not been the de-facto standard on public graph representation leaderboards [22, 14, 21]. There are many attempts of leveraging Transformer into the graph domain, but the only effective way is replacing some key modules (e.g., feature aggregation) in classic GNN variants by the softmax attention [50, 7, 23, 51, 61, 46, 13]. Therefore, it is still an open question whether Transformer architecture is suitable to model graphs and how to make it work in graph representation learning.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::51
retrieved_contexts.summary: There are several works that study the performance of pure Transformer architectures (stacked by transformer layers) with modifications on graph representation tasks, which are more related to our Graphormer. For example, several parts of the transformer layer are modified in [46], including an additional GNN employed in attention sub-layer to produce vectors of $Q$ , $K$ , and $V$ , long-range residual connection, and two branches of FFN to produce node and edge representations separately. They pre-train their model on 10 million unlabelled molecules and achieve excellent results by fine-tuning on downstream tasks. Attention module is modified to a soft adjacency matrix in [41] by directly adding the adjacency matrix and RDKit6-computed inter-atomic distance matrix to the attention probabilites. Very recently, Dwivedi et al. [13] revisit a series of works for Transformerbased GNNs, and suggest that the attention mechanism in Transformers on graph data should only aggregate the information from neighborhood (i.e., using adjacent matrix as attention mask) to ensure graph sparsity, and propose to use Laplacian eigenvector as positional encoding. Their model GT surpasses baseline GNNs on graph representation task. A concurrent work [28] propose a novel full Laplacian spectrum to learn the position of each node in a graph, and empirically shows better results than GT.

question: 对比 Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) 和 Graphormer (Ying et al., 2021) 中“层数选择”的考量。Transformer 使用 N=6 层，Graphormer 在 PCQM4M-LSC 上使用 12 层，在 MolPCBA 上使用 18 层。请分析为何 Graphormer 可以堆叠更深，而原始 Transformer 堆叠 6 层已足够？
ground_truth: 原始 Transformer 在机器翻译任务中 6 层已能取得 SOTA，因为翻译任务中语义信息相对密集，较深的层可能带来优化困难（Post-LN）且收益递减。Graphormer 使用 Pre-LN 优化更稳定，可以堆叠更深而不会出现梯度问题。此外，图任务（尤其是分子图）中节点数较少（平均约 15 个原子），深层网络可以逐步构建更抽象的分子表示，从原子级到基团级再到分子级，因此更深层能带来持续的性能提升（表 5 中 12 层优于 6 层）。
answer:
根据提供的检索文档，关于 Transformer 与 Graphormer 的层数选择及深度考量，现有信息如下：

**1. Graphormer 的层数配置与选择依据**
文档中明确记录了 Graphormer 在不同数据集上的层数设置。在 PCQM4M-LSC 数据集的消融实验中，为了节省计算资源，模型采用了 12 层结构 [4]。而在针对 MolPCBA 任务（基于 PCQM4M-LSC 预训练）的配置中，研究人员训练了包含 18 个 Transformer 层的深层 Graphormer 模型 [9]。
关于选择较深网络的原因，文档指出是出于实证经验：“我们发现 MolPCBA 的性能受益于较大的预训练模型规模”（Empirically, we find that the performance on MolPCBA benefits from the large pre-training model size），因此选择了 18 层 [9]。此外，Graphormer 在架构上对原始 Transformer 进行了优化，将层归一化（Layer Normalization）置于多头自注意力（MHA）和前馈网络（FFN）之前而非之后，这种修改被广泛采用以带来更有效的优化效果 [8]。

**2. 原始 Transformer 层数及对比分析的缺失**
尽管文档引用了原始 Transformer 的实现作为基础 [49] [8]，但提供的文本片段中并未提及原始 Transformer 具体使用 N=6 层的设定，也未提供关于“为何原始 Transformer 堆叠 6 层已足够”的解释性内容。同时，文档没有直接对比两种架构在“可堆叠深度”上的理论差异或能力边界，仅提到 Graphormer 的自注意力模块具有二次复杂度，限制了其在大图上的应用，但这主要涉及节点数量而非层数堆叠的限制 [5] [7]。

综上所述，现有文献仅能证实 Graphormer 因性能收益和预训练规模需求而采用了更深的设计（12 至 18 层），并提及了通过 Pre-LN 改进优化稳定性 [8]；但关于原始 Transformer 层数选择的理由以及两者在深度堆叠能力上的直接对比分析，**文献未提及**。
judge_reason: retrieved_contexts 未直接支持原始 Transformer 层数选择的关键事实，仅涉及 Graphormer 部分；answer 忠于上下文，准确总结未添加额外信息。

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::3
retrieved_contexts.summary: In this paper, we give an affirmative answer by developing Graphormer, which is directly built upon the standard Transformer, and achieves state-of-the-art performance on a wide range of graphlevel prediction tasks, including the very recent Open Graph Benchmark Large-Scale Challenge (OGB-LSC) [21], and several popular leaderboards (e.g., OGB [22], Benchmarking-GNN [14]). The Transformer is originally designed for sequence modeling. To utilize its power in graphs, we believe the key is to properly incorporate structural information of graphs into the model. Note that for each node $i$ , the self-attention only calculates the semantic similarity between $i$ and other nodes, without considering the structural information of a graph reflected on the nodes and the relation between node pairs. Graphormer incorporates several effective structural encoding methods to leverage such information, which are described below.

retrieved_contexts.summary: The Transformer [49] is well acknowledged as the most powerful neural network in modelling sequential data, such as natural language [11, 35, 6] and speech [17]. Model variants built upon Transformer have also been shown great performance in computer vision [12, 36] and programming language [19, 63, 44]. However, to the best of our knowledge, Transformer has still not been the de-facto standard on public graph representation leaderboards [22, 14, 21]. There are many attempts of leveraging Transformer into the graph domain, but the only effective way is replacing some key modules (e.g., feature aggregation) in classic GNN variants by the softmax attention [50, 7, 23, 51, 61, 46, 13]. Therefore, it is still an open question whether Transformer architecture is suitable to model graphs and how to make it work in graph representation learning.

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::1
retrieved_contexts.summary: The Transformer architecture has become a dominant choice in many domains, such as natural language processing and computer vision. Yet, it has not achieved competitive performance on popular leaderboards of graph-level prediction compared to mainstream GNN variants. Therefore, it remains a mystery how Transformers could perform well for graph representation learning. In this paper, we solve this mystery by presenting Graphormer, which is built upon the standard Transformer architecture, and could attain excellent results on a broad range of graph representation learning tasks, especially on the recent OGB Large-Scale Challenge. Our key insight to utilizing Transformer in the graph is the necessity of effectively encoding the structural information of a graph into the model. To this end, we propose several simple yet effective structural encoding methods to help Graphormer better model graph-structured data. Besides, we mathematically characterize the expressive power of Graphormer and exhibit that with our ways of encoding the structural information of graphs, many popular GNN variants could be covered as the special cases of Graphormer. The code and models of Graphormer will be made publicly available at https://github.com/Microsoft/Graphormer.

question: 对比 ViT (Dosovitskiy et al., 2021) 和 Graphormer (Ying et al., 2021) 在“处理输入异构性”上的能力。ViT 处理的是同构的像素网格（图像），所有输入具有相同的结构；Graphormer 处理的是异构的图结构（节点数、边数、拓扑结构均可变）。请分析两者在处理变长、变结构输入时的灵活性差异。
ground_truth: ViT 通过固定 patch 大小和固定分辨率来统一输入尺寸，对于不同尺寸的图像需要 resize 或调整位置嵌入插值，本质上要求输入结构相对规整。Graphormer 天然支持任意节点数、任意边数和任意拓扑结构的图，因为自注意力计算不依赖于固定的序列长度，空间编码（SPD）对任何连通性都有效，边编码也适用于任意路径。因此 Graphormer 在处理异构输入方面更具灵活性，这是图数据本身的特性决定的。
answer:
根据检索到的本地知识库内容，ViT 与 Graphormer 在处理输入结构及异构性方面存在显著差异，具体分析如下：

**1. 输入数据的结构性假设差异**
ViT 将图像解释为补丁（patches）的序列进行处理，其架构除初始补丁提取步骤外，未引入特定的图像归纳偏置 [10]。相比之下，Graphormer 明确指出图结构与语言、图像等其他结构化数据不同，不存在用于嵌入图的规范网格（canonical grid），节点仅位于非欧几里得空间中并通过边连接 [1]。因此，Graphormer 中的节点并非按序列排列，无法直接沿用处理序列的标准方法 [2]。

**2. 空间关系与位置编码的处理机制**
在 ViT 中，二维邻域结构的使用非常有限，仅在模型开始时通过切分图像为补丁体现，且初始化时的位置嵌入不包含关于补丁 2D 位置的信息，所有空间关系需从头学习 [7]。虽然 ViT 可通过调整位置嵌入来适应不同分辨率的图像，但这主要涉及序列长度的变化 [7]。此外，ViT 有修改版本通过轴向注意力（Axial Attention）块来处理二维形状输入，即行自注意力加列自注意力的组合 [3]。
相反，Graphormer 为了捕捉图中节点间的结构关系，提出了新颖的空间编码（Spatial Encoding）。该机制基于节点对之间的最短路径距离作为空间关系的度量，并将其作为偏置项加入 softmax 注意力中，以准确捕获图中的空间依赖 [1]。对于不连通节点，则设定特殊值进行区分 [2]。

**3. 异构特征与边信息的利用**
Graphormer 专门设计了边编码方法来处理图中包含的额外空间信息，例如分子图中原子间键的类型 [1]。它计算沿最短路径上边特征与可学习嵌入的点积平均值，并将其用于注意力模块，从而更好地建模节点对之间的关系 [1]。这种设计使得 Graphormer 能够整合节点特征与边特征，而传统的聚合方式可能无法有效利用整个图的边信息 [9]。ViT 的相关描述中未提及类似的针对异构边特征的编码机制，主要关注于补丁序列的处理 [10]。

**4. 变长与规模扩展性的限制**
关于处理变长输入的灵活性，ViT 支持通过微调阶段调整位置嵌入以适应不同分辨率的图像 [7]。然而，Graphormer 在处理大规模图时面临挑战，其自注意力模块的二次复杂度限制了其在大型图上的应用，需要未来的开发以提高效率 [5]。这表明尽管 Graphormer 能处理拓扑结构多变的图，但在处理极大规模的变长输入时存在计算复杂度的约束 [5]。

综上所述，ViT 倾向于将图像视为具有固定网格结构的序列，通过位置嵌入适应尺寸变化；而 Graphormer 则针对无规范网格的图结构，显式编码节点间的空间与边关系，但受限于计算复杂度。
judge_reason: 检索到的上下文直接支持ViT和Graphormer在处理异构输入的关键差异；回答基于上下文内容，没有明显添加外部常识。

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::5
retrieved_contexts.summary: Second, we propose a novel Spatial Encoding in Graphormer to capture the structural relation between nodes. One notable geometrical property that distinguishes graph-structured data from other structured data, e.g., language, images, is that there does not exist a canonical grid to embed the graph. In fact, nodes can only lie in a non-Euclidean space and are linked by edges. To model such structural information, for each node pair, we assign a learnable embedding based on their spatial relation. Multiple measurements in the literature could be leveraged for modeling spatial relations. For a general purpose, we use the distance of the shortest path between any two nodes as a demonstration, which will be encoded as a bias term in the softmax attention and help the model accurately capture the spatial dependency in a graph. In addition, sometimes there is additional spatial information contained in edge features, such as the type of bond between two atoms in a molecular graph. We design a new edge encoding method to further take such signal into the Transformer layers. To be concrete, for each node pair, we compute an average of dot-products of the edge features and learnable embeddings along the shortest path, then use it in the attention module. Equipped with these encodings, Graphormer could better model the relationship for node pairs and represent the graph.

To encode the structural information of a graph in the model, we propose a novel Spatial Encoding. Concretely, for any graph $G$ , we consider a function $\phi \left( v _ { i } , v _ { j } \right) : V \times V \to \mathbb { R }$ which measures the spatial relation between $v _ { i }$ and $v _ { j }$ in graph $G$ . The function $\phi$ can be defined by the connectivity between the nodes in the graph. In this paper, we choose $\phi ( v _ { i } , v _ { j } )$ to be the distance of the shortest path (SPD) between $v _ { i }$ and $v _ { j }$ if the two nodes are connected. If not, we set the output of $\phi$ to be a special value, i.e., -1. We assign each (feasible) output value a learnable scalar which will serve as a bias term in the self-attention module. retrieved_contexts.summary: Inductive bias. We note that Vision Transformer has much less image-specific inductive bias than CNNs. In CNNs, locality, two-dimensional neighborhood structure, and translation equivariance are baked into each layer throughout the whole model. In ViT, only MLP layers are local and translationally equivariant, while the self-attention layers are global. The two-dimensional neighborhood structure is used very sparingly: in the beginning of the model by cutting the image into patches and at fine-tuning time for adjusting the position embeddings for images of different resolution (as described below). Other than that, the position embeddings at initialization time carry no information about the 2D positions of the patches and all spatial relations between the patches have to be learned from scratch.

ground_truth: 文献未提及。
answer:
根据提供的检索内容，文献未提及名为“自适应注意力跨度”（Adaptive Attention Span）的机制。

文档中关于注意力机制的描述主要集中在以下方面：
1.  **全局依赖建模**：原文档指出注意力机制允许对输入或输出序列中的依赖进行建模，而不受其在序列中距离的影响 [1]。
2.  **未来研究方向**：在结论部分，作者提到计划调查“局部、受限的注意力机制”（local, restricted attention mechanisms）以高效处理图像、音频和视频等大输入，但这被表述为未来的工作计划而非已提出的机制 [9]。

因此，基于现有检索材料，无法确认 Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) 中提出了“自适应注意力跨度”这一具体机制。

retrieved_contexts.summary:
Attention mechanisms have become an integral part of compelling sequence modeling and transduction models in various tasks, allowing modeling of dependencies without regard to their distance in the input or output sequences [2, 19]. In all but a few cases [27], however, such attention mechanisms are used in conjunction with a recurrent network.

In this work we propose the Transformer, a model architecture eschewing recurrence and instead relying entirely on an attention mechanism to draw global dependencies between input and output. The Transformer allows for significantly more parallelization and can reach a new state of the art in translation quality after being trained for as little as twelve hours on eight P100 GPUs.

retrieved_contexts.summary: Axial Attention (Huang et al., 2020; Ho et al., 2019) is a simple, yet effective technique to run selfattention on large inputs that are organized as multidimensional tensors. The general idea of axial attention is to perform multiple attention operations, each along a single axis of the input tensor, instead of applying 1-dimensional attention to the flattened version of the input. In axial attention, each attention mixes information along a particular axis, while keeping information along the other axes independent. Along this line, Wang et al. (2020b) proposed the AxialResNet model in which all the convolutions with kernel size $3 \times 3$ in a ResNet50 are replaced by axial self-attention, i.e. a row and column attention, augmented by relative positional encoding. question: 在 Vision Transformer (ViT) (Dosovitskiy et al., 2021) 中，作者提出了一种“动态 patch 划分”（Dynamic Patch Partitioning）策略，根据图像内容自适应地调整 patch 的大小和形状，从而在保持性能的同时减少计算量。请问这是 ViT 的核心创新之一吗？

提供的文档主要描述了以下关于 Patch 划分的处理方式：
1.  作者将图像分割为 patches，并将这些 patches 的线性嵌入序列作为 Transformer 的输入 [1]。
2.  除了初始的 patch 提取步骤外，架构中未引入特定的图像归纳偏置，而是将图像解释为 patches 序列并通过标准 Transformer 编码器处理 [8]。
3.  在相关工作中，对比了其他模型使用的 patch 大小（如 $2 \times 2$），暗示本文采用的是不同的标准处理方式以适配中等分辨率图像 [10]。

综上所述，基于现有检索证据，无法确认“动态 patch 划分”是 ViT 的核心创新，文中强调的是直接应用 Transformer 并进行最少的修改 [1]。
judge_reason: 检索到的上下文直接显示ViT文献未提及动态patch划分，回答基于上下文正确指出这一事实且未添加额外信息。

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::4
retrieved_contexts.summary: Inspired by the Transformer scaling successes in NLP, we experiment with applying a standard Transformer directly to images, with the fewest possible modifications. To do so, we split an image into patches and provide the sequence of linear embeddings of these patches as an input to a Transformer. Image patches are treated the same way as tokens (words) in an NLP application. We train the model on image classification in supervised fashion.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::101
retrieved_contexts.summary: Moreover, we have modified ViT to process inputs in the 2-dimensional shape, instead of a 1- dimensional sequence of patches, and incorporate Axial Transformer blocks, in which instead of a self-attention followed by an MLP, we have a a row-self-attention plus an MLP followed by a column-self-attention plus an MLP.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::20
retrieved_contexts.summary: Hybrid Architecture. As an alternative to raw image patches, the input sequence can be formed from feature maps of a CNN (LeCun et al., 1989). In this hybrid model, the patch embedding projection $\mathbf { E }$ (Eq. 1) is applied to patches extracted from a CNN feature map. As a special case, the patches can have spatial size 1x1, which means that the input sequence is obtained by simply flattening the spatial dimensions of the feature map and projecting to the Transformer dimension. The classification input embedding and position embeddings are added as described above.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::19
retrieved_contexts.summary: Inductive bias. We note that Vision Transformer has much less image-specific inductive bias than CNNs. In CNNs, locality, two-dimensional neighborhood structure, and translation equivariance are baked into each layer throughout the whole model. In ViT, only MLP layers are local and translationally equivariant, while the self-attention layers are global. The two-dimensional neighborhood structure is used very sparingly: in the beginning of the model by cutting the image into patches and at fine-tuning time for adjusting the position embeddings for images of different resolution (as described below). Other than that, the position embeddings at initialization time carry no information about the 2D positions of the patches and all spatial relations between the patches have to be learned from scratch.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::11
retrieved_contexts.summary: Another recent related model is image GPT (iGPT) (Chen et al., 2020a), which applies Transformers to image pixels after reducing image resolution and color space. The model is trained in an unsupervised fashion as a generative model, and the resulting representation can then be fine-tuned or probed linearly for classification performance, achieving a maximal accuracy of $72 \%$ on ImageNet.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::46
retrieved_contexts.summary: To begin to understand how the Vision Transformer processes image data, we analyze its internal representations. The first layer of the Vision Transformer linearly projects the flattened patches into a lower-dimensional space (Eq. 1). Figure 7 (left) shows the top principal components of the the learned embedding filters. The components resemble plausible basis functions for a low-dimensional representation of the fine structure within each patch.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::52
retrieved_contexts.summary: We have explored the direct application of Transformers to image recognition. Unlike prior works using self-attention in computer vision, we do not introduce image-specific inductive biases into the architecture apart from the initial patch extraction step. Instead, we interpret an image as a sequence of patches and process it by a standard Transformer encoder as used in NLP. This simple, yet scalable, strategy works surprisingly well when coupled with pre-training on large datasets. Thus, Vision Transformer matches or exceeds the state of the art on many image classification datasets, whilst being relatively cheap to pre-train.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

question: Graphormer (Ying et al., 2021) 中，作者提出了一种“谱注意力机制”（Spectral Attention），通过引入图的拉普拉斯矩阵的特征值作为全局结构编码，以增强模型对图频谱特性的感知能力。请问这是 Graphormer 的空间编码方法吗？
ground_truth: 文献未提及。
answer:
根据提供的检索内容，关于 Graphormer 的编码机制描述如下：

Graphormer 提出了三种新颖的图结构编码（graph structural encodings），作为神经网络的归纳偏置以学习图表示 [6]。其中明确提到的编码方法包括用于注意力层的边编码（Edge Encoding），该方法通过计算节点对之间最短路径上的边特征点积平均值来增强模型对边缘特征的感知 [3]。此外，Graphormer 还引入了中心性编码（Centrality Encoding），利用节点在图中的重要性信号（如度数）来补充注意力计算中缺失的信息 [7]。

关于您提到的“谱注意力机制”及拉普拉斯矩阵特征值，提供的文档并未将其列为 Graphormer 的编码方法。相反，文档在讨论图上的位置编码时指出，“绝对拉普拉斯位置编码（Absolute Laplacian PE）”被引用文献 [13]（Dwivedi et al.）所采用，而非直接归属于 Graphormer [9]。在参考文献列表中，标题包含"Spectral Attention"的论文是 Kreuzer 等人于 2021 年发表的《Rethinking graph transformers with spectral attention》（参考文献 [28]），这进一步表明该术语可能指向其他研究工作 [8]。因此，基于现有文献片段，无法确认 Graphormer 使用了谱注意力机制或拉普拉斯特征值作为其空间/结构编码。
judge_reason: 检索上下文直接展示了Graphormer的编码方法（如边编码和中心性编码），并指出谱注意力机制属于其他文献，从而支持答案的关键事实。

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::23
retrieved_contexts.summary: To better encode edge features into attention layers, we propose a new edge encoding method in Graphormer. The attention mechanism needs to estimate correlations for each node pair $( v _ { i } , v _ { j } )$ , and we believe the edges connecting them should be considered in the correlation as in [34, 51]. For each ordered node pair $( v _ { i } , v _ { j } )$ , we find (one of) the shortest path ${ \bf S P } _ { i j } = ( e _ { 1 } , e _ { 2 } , . . . , e _ { N } )$ from $v _ { i }$ to $v _ { j }$ , and compute an average of the dot-products of the edge feature and a learnable embedding along the path. The proposed edge encoding incorporates edge features via a bias term to the attention module. Concretely, we modify the $( i , j )$ -element of $A$ in Eq. (3) further with the edge encoding $c _ { i j }$ as:

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::5
retrieved_contexts.summary:
Attention mechanisms have become an integral part of compelling sequence modeling and transduction models in various tasks, allowing modeling of dependencies without regard to their distance in the input or output sequences [2, 19]. In all but a few cases [27], however, such attention mechanisms are used in conjunction with a recurrent network.

In this work we propose the Transformer, a model architecture eschewing recurrence and instead relying entirely on an attention mechanism to draw global dependencies between input and output. The Transformer allows for significantly more parallelization and can reach a new state of the art in translation quality after being trained for as little as twelve hours on eight P100 GPUs.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf

Many of the attention heads attend to a distant dependency of the verb ‘making’, completing the phrase ‘making...more difficult’. Attentions here shown only for the word ‘making’. Different colors represent different heads. Best viewed in color.

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::55
retrieved_contexts.summary: We have explored the direct application of Transformers to graph representation. With three novel graph structural encodings, the proposed Graphormer works surprisingly well on a wide range of popular benchmark datasets. While these initial results are encouraging, many challenges remain. For example, the quadratic complexity of the self-attention module restricts Graphormer’s application on large graphs. Therefore, future development of efficient Graphormer is necessary. Performance improvement could be expected by leveraging domain knowledge-powered encodings on particular graph datasets. Finally, an applicable graph sampling strategy is desired for node representation extraction with Graphormer. We leave them for future works.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::15
retrieved_contexts.summary: In Eq.4, the attention distribution is calculated based on the semantic correlation between nodes. However, node centrality, which measures how important a node is in the graph, is usually a strong signal for graph understanding. For example, celebrities who have a huge number of followers are important factors in predicting the trend of a social network [40, 39]. Such information is neglected in the current attention calculation, and we believe it should be a valuable signal for Transformer models.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::7
retrieved_contexts.summary: Transformers were proposed by Vaswani et al. (2017) for machine translation, and have since become the state of the art method in many NLP tasks. Large Transformer-based models are often pre-trained on large corpora and then fine-tuned for the task at hand: BERT (Devlin et al., 2019) uses a denoising self-supervised pre-training task, while the GPT line of work uses language modeling as its pre-training task (Radford et al., 2018; 2019; Brown et al., 2020).
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::2
retrieved_contexts.summary: The Transformer [49] is well acknowledged as the most powerful neural network in modelling sequential data, such as natural language [11, 35, 6] and speech [17]. Model variants built upon Transformer have also been shown great performance in computer vision [12, 36] and programming language [19, 63, 44]. However, to the best of our knowledge, Transformer has still not been the de-facto standard on public graph representation leaderboards [22, 14, 21]. There are many attempts of leveraging Transformer into the graph domain, but the only effective way is replacing some key modules (e.g., feature aggregation) in classic GNN variants by the softmax attention [50, 7, 23, 51, 61, 46, 13]. Therefore, it is still an open question whether Transformer architecture is suitable to model graphs and how to make it work in graph representation learning.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::8
retrieved_contexts.summary: To the best of our knowledge, however, the Transformer is the first transduction model relying entirely on self-attention to compute representations of its input and output without using sequencealigned RNNs or convolution. In the following sections, we will describe the Transformer, motivate self-attention and discuss its advantages over models such as [17, 18] and [9].
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::53
retrieved_contexts.summary:
In this work, we presented the Transformer, the first sequence transduction model based entirely on attention, replacing the recurrent layers most commonly used in encoder-decoder architectures with multi-headed self-attention.

For translation tasks, the Transformer can be trained significantly faster than architectures based on recurrent or convolutional layers. On both WMT 2014 English-to-German and WMT 2014 English-to-French translation tasks, we achieve a new state of the art. In the former task our best model outperforms even all previously reported ensembles.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::1
retrieved_contexts.summary: The Transformer architecture has become a dominant choice in many domains, such as natural language processing and computer vision. Yet, it has not achieved competitive performance on popular leaderboards of graph-level prediction compared to mainstream GNN variants. Therefore, it remains a mystery how Transformers could perform well for graph representation learning. In this paper, we solve this mystery by presenting Graphormer, which is built upon the standard Transformer architecture, and could attain excellent results on a broad range of graph representation learning tasks, especially on the recent OGB Large-Scale Challenge. Our key insight to utilizing Transformer in the graph is the necessity of effectively encoding the structural information of a graph into the model. To this end, we propose several simple yet effective structural encoding methods to help Graphormer better model graph-structured data. Besides, we mathematically characterize the expressive power of Graphormer and exhibit that with our ways of encoding the structural information of graphs, many popular GNN variants could be covered as the special cases of Graphormer. The code and models of Graphormer will be made publicly available at https://github.com/Microsoft/Graphormer.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::14
retrieved_contexts.summary: In model design we follow the original Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) as closely as possible. An advantage of this intentionally simple setup is that scalable NLP Transformer architectures – and their efficient implementations – can be used almost out of the box.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::1
retrieved_contexts.summary: While the Transformer architecture has become the de-facto standard for natural language processing tasks, its applications to computer vision remain limited. In vision, attention is either applied in conjunction with convolutional networks, or used to replace certain components of convolutional networks while keeping their overall structure in place. We show that this reliance on CNNs is not necessary and a pure transformer applied directly to sequences of image patches can perform very well on image classification tasks. When pre-trained on large amounts of data and transferred to multiple mid-sized or small image recognition benchmarks (ImageNet, CIFAR-100, VTAB, etc.), Vision Transformer (ViT) attains excellent results compared to state-of-the-art convolutional networks while requiring substantially fewer computational resources to train.1
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::2
retrieved_contexts.summary: Self-attention-based architectures, in particular Transformers (Vaswani et al., 2017), have become the model of choice in natural language processing (NLP). The dominant approach is to pre-train on a large text corpus and then fine-tune on a smaller task-specific dataset (Devlin et al., 2019). Thanks to Transformers’ computational efficiency and scalability, it has become possible to train models of unprecedented size, with over 100B parameters (Brown et al., 2020; Lepikhin et al., 2020). With the models and datasets growing, there is still no sign of saturating performance.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

question: 在 Vision Transformer (ViT) (Dosovitskiy et al., 2021) 中，作者将图像划分为 8x8 的 patch 作为标准配置，并论证了 8x8 的 patch 大小在性能和计算效率之间达到了最优平衡。请问 ViT 默认使用的 patch 大小是 8x8 吗？
ground_truth: 文献未提及。
answer: 根据提供的检索内容，文档中并未提及将 8x8 作为 Vision Transformer (ViT) 的标准配置或最优平衡点。相反，检索到的片段中记录了不同的 patch 大小设置：在针对 Transformer 架构维度缩放的消融实验中，所有配置均基于 patch size 为 32 的 ViT 模型 [1]。此外，在关于位置嵌入的消融研究摘要中，明确提到了使用 ViT-B/16 模型，并通过示例数据（如 $224 \times 224$ 图像对应 $14 \times 14$ 序列）暗示了该模型使用的是 16x16 的 patch 大小 [9]。因此，基于现有知识库内容，无法支持“默认 patch 大小为 8x8"的说法。
judge_reason: 检索上下文显示patch大小为32，直接反驳了8x8的说法；但回答中关于ViT-B/16暗示16x16的部分在上下文中未明确支持。

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::87
retrieved_contexts.summary: We ran ablations on scaling different dimensions of the Transformer architecture to find out which are best suited for scaling to very large models. Figure 8 shows 5-shot performance on ImageNet for different configurations. All configurations are based on a ViT model with 8 layers, $D = 1 0 2 4$ , $D _ { M L P } = 2 0 4 8$ and a patch size of 32, the intersection of all lines. We can see that scaling the depth results in the biggest improvements which are clearly visible up until 64 layers. However, diminishing returns are already visible after 16 layers. Interestingly, scaling the width of the network seems to result in the smallest changes. Decreasing the patch size and thus increasing the effective sequence length shows surprisingly robust improvements without introducing parameters. These findings suggest that compute might be a better predictor of performance than the number of parameters, and that scaling should emphasize depth over width if any. Overall, we find that scaling all dimensions proportionally results in robust improvements.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.summary: Inspired by the Transformer scaling successes in NLP, we experiment with applying a standard Transformer directly to images, with the fewest possible modifications. To do so, we split an image into patches and provide the sequence of linear embeddings of these patches as an input to a Transformer. Image patches are treated the same way as tokens (words) in an NLP application. We train the model on image classification in supervised fashion.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::19
retrieved_contexts.summary: Inductive bias. We note that Vision Transformer has much less image-specific inductive bias than CNNs. In CNNs, locality, two-dimensional neighborhood structure, and translation equivariance are baked into each layer throughout the whole model. In ViT, only MLP layers are local and translationally equivariant, while the self-attention layers are global. The two-dimensional neighborhood structure is used very sparingly: in the beginning of the model by cutting the image into patches and at fine-tuning time for adjusting the position embeddings for images of different resolution (as described below). Other than that, the position embeddings at initialization time carry no information about the 2D positions of the patches and all spatial relations between the patches have to be learned from scratch.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::20
retrieved_contexts.summary: Hybrid Architecture. As an alternative to raw image patches, the input sequence can be formed from feature maps of a CNN (LeCun et al., 1989). In this hybrid model, the patch embedding projection $\mathbf { E }$ (Eq. 1) is applied to patches extracted from a CNN feature map. As a special case, the patches can have spatial size 1x1, which means that the input sequence is obtained by simply flattening the spatial dimensions of the feature map and projecting to the Transformer dimension. The classification input embedding and position embeddings are added as described above.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

Figure 1: Model overview. We split an image into fixed-size patches, linearly embed each of them, add position embeddings, and feed the resulting sequence of vectors to a standard Transformer encoder. In order to perform classification, we use the standard approach of adding an extra learnable “classification token” to the sequence. The illustration of the Transformer encoder was inspired by Vaswani et al. (2017).

question: Graphormer (Ying et al., 2021) 中，作者提出了一种“可微分图池化”（Differentiable Graph Pooling）模块，通过学习软分配矩阵将节点聚类为超节点，从而降低大图的计算复杂度。请问这是 Graphormer 处理大规模图的核心技术吗？
ground_truth: 文献未提及。
answer:
根据提供的检索证据，文档中**未提及**Graphormer 采用了“可微分图池化”模块作为处理大规模图的核心技术。相反，文档详细描述了 Graphormer 通过引入一个特殊节点（[VNode]）来代表整个图的嵌入，该节点与图中每个节点单独连接 [1]。在最终的聚合步骤中，整个图的表示 $h_G$ 被定义为 [VNode] 的特征 [1]。

此外，关于大规模图的处理，文档明确指出自注意力机制的二次复杂度限制了 Graphormer 在大型图上的应用，并强调需要未来的开发以提高效率，而非依赖池化策略 [3][7]。文档还提到 Graphormer 的核心设计在于三种新颖的图结构编码（如空间编码），以捕捉节点间的结构关系 [5]。因此，基于现有资料，Graphormer 处理图表示的核心机制是特殊节点与结构编码，而非图池化 [6]。
judge_reason: 检索上下文直接描述了Graphormer的特殊节点和结构编码，未提及可微分图池化，答案基于此推断并忠实于证据。

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::27
retrieved_contexts.summary: Special Node. As stated in the previous section, various graph pooling functions are proposed to represent the graph embedding. Inspired by [15], in Graphormer, we add a special node called [VNode] to the graph, and make connection between [VNode] and each node individually. In the AGGREGATE-COMBINE step, the representation of [VNode] has been updated as normal nodes in graph, and the representation of the entire graph $h _ { G }$ would be the node feature of [VNode] in the final layer. In the BERT model [11, 35], there is a similar token, i.e., [CLS], which is a special token attached at the beginning of each sequence, to represent the sequence-level feature on downstream tasks. While the [VNode] is connected to all other nodes in graph, which means the distance of the shortest path is 1 for any $\phi ( [ \mathtt { V N o d e } ] , v _ { j } )$ and $\phi ( v _ { i } , [ \mathtt { V N o d e } ] )$ , the connection is not physical. To distinguish the connection of physical and virtual, inspired by [25], we reset all spatial encodings for $b _ { \phi ( [ \tt N N o d e ] , \boldsymbol { v } _ { j } ) }$ and $b _ { \phi ( v _ { i } , [ \mathsf { V N o d e } ] ) }$ to a distinct learnable scalar.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::55
retrieved_contexts.summary: We have explored the direct application of Transformers to graph representation. With three novel graph structural encodings, the proposed Graphormer works surprisingly well on a wide range of popular benchmark datasets. While these initial results are encouraging, many challenges remain. For example, the quadratic complexity of the self-attention module restricts Graphormer’s application on large graphs. Therefore, future development of efficient Graphormer is necessary. Performance improvement could be expected by leveraging domain knowledge-powered encodings on particular graph datasets. Finally, an applicable graph sampling strategy is desired for node representation extraction with Graphormer. We leave them for future works.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::13
retrieved_contexts.summary: In this section, we present our Graphormer for graph tasks. First, we elaborate on several key designs in the Graphormer, which serve as an inductive bias in the neural network to learn the graph representation. We further provide the detailed implementations of Graphormer. Finally, we show that our proposed Graphormer is more powerful since popular GNN models [26, 54, 18] are its special cases.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::5
retrieved_contexts.summary: Second, we propose a novel Spatial Encoding in Graphormer to capture the structural relation between nodes. One notable geometrical property that distinguishes graph-structured data from other structured data, e.g., language, images, is that there does not exist a canonical grid to embed the graph. In fact, nodes can only lie in a non-Euclidean space and are linked by edges. To model such structural information, for each node pair, we assign a learnable embedding based on their spatial relation. Multiple measurements in the literature could be leveraged for modeling spatial relations. For a general purpose, we use the distance of the shortest path between any two nodes as a demonstration, which will be encoded as a bias term in the softmax attention and help the model accurately capture the spatial dependency in a graph. In addition, sometimes there is additional spatial information contained in edge features, such as the type of bond between two atoms in a molecular graph. We design a new edge encoding method to further take such signal into the Transformer layers. To be concrete, for each node pair, we compute an average of dot-products of the edge features and learnable embeddings along the shortest path, then use it in the attention module. Equipped with these encodings, Graphormer could better model the relationship for node pairs and represent the graph.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::32
retrieved_contexts.summary:
Fact 2. By choosing proper weights, every node representation of the output of a Graphormer layer without additional encodings can represent MEAN READOUT functions.

This fact takes the advantage of self-attention that each node can attend to all other nodes. Thus it can simulate graph-level READOUT operation to aggregate information from the whole graph. Besides the theoretical justification, we empirically find that Graphormer does not encounter the problem of over-smoothing, which makes the improvement scalable. The fact also inspires us to introduce a special node for graph readout (see the previous subsection).
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::89
retrieved_contexts.summary: Complexity. Similar to regular Transformer, the attention mechanism in Graphormer scales quadratically with the number of nodes $n$ in the input graph, which may be prohibitively expensive for large $n$ and precludes its usage in settings with limited computational resources. Recently, many solutions have been proposed to address this problem in Transformer [25, 52, 57, 37]. This issue would be greatly benefit from the future development of efficient Graphormer.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::30
retrieved_contexts.summary: Moreover, we show further that by using our spatial encoding, Graphormer can go beyond classic message passing GNNs whose expressive power is no more than the 1-Weisfeiler-Lehman (WL) test. We give a concrete example in Appendix A to show how Graphormer helps distinguish graphs that the 1-WL test fails to.

question: Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) 的作者在附录中提供了关于“相对位置编码”（Relative Position Encoding）的完整推导，并证明相对位置编码在机器翻译任务中显著优于绝对位置编码。请问这是 Transformer 的最终选择吗？
ground_truth: 文献未提及。
answer:
根据检索到的本地知识库内容，关于 Transformer 中位置编码的选择及 Vaswani 等人（2017）的具体结论，现有文档并未直接验证“附录中提供了相对位置编码的完整推导并证明其显著优于绝对位置编码”这一具体细节，也未明确声明相对位置编码是 Transformer 架构的最终唯一选择。

不过，检索资料中提供了关于 Transformer 位置编码类型的通用描述与对比信息：
1. 对于序列数据，Transformer 层中可以显式指定不同位置或编码位置依赖关系，既可以为每个位置提供嵌入（即绝对位置编码），也可以编码任意两个位置之间的相对距离（即相对位置编码）[1]。这表明在相关技术讨论中，绝对与相对位置编码均被视为可选方案。
2. 在视觉 Transformer（ViT）的消融研究中，结果显示有无位置嵌入的性能差距较大，但不同位置编码方式之间的性能差异很小或几乎没有差异 [4]。这暗示在不同应用场景下，位置编码策略的选择可能并非决定性的单一标准。
3. 尽管 Vaswani 等人提出的 Transformer 在机器翻译任务上取得了显著成果 [3][6]，但提供的文档片段中未包含关于该原始论文附录中具体推导过程或其对位置编码最终选择的明确定论。

综上所述，基于当前检索到的文献，无法确认相对位置编码是否为 Transformer 的最终固定选择，现有资料仅表明两者均为处理位置信息的可行方法，且在某些变体中差异并不显著。
judge_reason: 检索到的上下文未提及Vaswani等人附录中的推导和比较，不支持关键事实；答案基于上下文内容，没有引入额外信息。

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::18
retrieved_contexts.summary: An advantage of Transformer is its global receptive field. In each Transformer layer, each token can attend to the information at any position and then process its representation. But this operation has a byproduct problem that the model has to explicitly specify different positions or encode the positional dependency (such as locality) in the layers. For sequential data, one can either give each position an embedding (i.e., absolute positional encoding [49]) as the input or encode the relative distance of any two positions (i.e., relative positional encoding [45, 47]) in the Transformer layer.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::53
retrieved_contexts.summary: Positional Encoding in Transformer on Graph. Several works introduce positional encoding (PE) to Transformer-based GNNs to help the model capture the node position information. For example, Graph-BERT [61] introduces three types of PE to embed the node position information to model, i.e., an absolute WL-PE which represents different nodes labeled by Weisfeiler-Lehman algorithm, an intimacy based PE and a hop based PE which are both variant to the sampled subgraphs. Absolute Laplacian PE is employed in [13] and empircal study shows that its performance surpasses the absolute WL-PE used in [61].

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::53
retrieved_contexts.summary:
In this work, we presented the Transformer, the first sequence transduction model based entirely on attention, replacing the recurrent layers most commonly used in encoder-decoder architectures with multi-headed self-attention.

For translation tasks, the Transformer can be trained significantly faster than architectures based on recurrent or convolutional layers. On both WMT 2014 English-to-German and WMT 2014 English-to-French translation tasks, we achieve a new state of the art. In the former task our best model outperforms even all previously reported ensembles.

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::95
retrieved_contexts.summary: Table 8 summarizes the results from this ablation study on a ViT-B/16 model. As we can see, while there is a large gap between the performances of the model with no positional embedding and models with positional embedding, there is little to no difference between different ways of encoding positional information. We speculate that since our Transformer encoder operates on patch-level inputs, as opposed to pixel-level, the differences in how to encode spatial information is less important. More precisely, in patch-level inputs, the spatial dimensions are much smaller than the original pixel-level inputs, e.g., $1 4 \times 1 4$ as opposed to $2 2 4 \times 2 2 4$ , and learning to represent the spatial relations in this resolution is equally easy for these different positional encoding strategies. Even so, the specific pattern of position embedding similarity learned by the network depends on the training hyperparameters (Figure 10).

retrieved_contexts.summary: Transformers were proposed by Vaswani et al. (2017) for machine translation, and have since become the state of the art method in many NLP tasks. Large Transformer-based models are often pre-trained on large corpora and then fine-tuned for the task at hand: BERT (Devlin et al., 2019) uses a denoising self-supervised pre-training task, while the GPT line of work uses language modeling as its pre-training task (Radford et al., 2018; 2019; Brown et al., 2020).

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::2
retrieved_contexts.summary: The Transformer [49] is well acknowledged as the most powerful neural network in modelling sequential data, such as natural language [11, 35, 6] and speech [17]. Model variants built upon Transformer have also been shown great performance in computer vision [12, 36] and programming language [19, 63, 44]. However, to the best of our knowledge, Transformer has still not been the de-facto standard on public graph representation leaderboards [22, 14, 21]. There are many attempts of leveraging Transformer into the graph domain, but the only effective way is replacing some key modules (e.g., feature aggregation) in classic GNN variants by the softmax attention [50, 7, 23, 51, 61, 46, 13]. Therefore, it is still an open question whether Transformer architecture is suitable to model graphs and how to make it work in graph representation learning.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::46
retrieved_contexts.summary: Node Relation Encoding. We compare previously used positional encoding (PE) to our proposed spatial encoding, which both aim to encode the information of distinct node relation to Transformers. There are various PEs employed by previous Transformer-based GNNs, e.g., Weisfeiler-Lehman-PE (WL-PE) [61] and Laplacian PE [3, 14]. We report the performance for Laplacian PE since it performs well comparing to a series of PEs for Graph Transformer in previous literature [13]. Transformer architecture with the spatial encoding outperforms the counterpart built on the positional encoding, which demonstrates the effectiveness of using spatial encoding to capture the node spatial information.

question: 在 Vision Transformer (ViT) (Dosovitskiy et al., 2021) 中，作者发现使用“卷积位置嵌入”（Convolutional Position Embedding）可以显著提升模型在小数据集上的性能，因此将其作为 ViT 的默认配置。请问 ViT 是否采用了卷积位置嵌入？
ground_truth: 文献未提及。
answer:
根据检索到的文档内容，Vision Transformer (ViT) 并未将“卷积位置嵌入”作为默认配置。相反，作者在模型中实际使用的是标准的可学习一维位置嵌入（standard learnable 1D position embeddings）[6]。文中明确指出，由于未观察到使用更先进的二维感知位置嵌入（2D-aware position embeddings）能带来显著的性能提升，因此保留了标准的一维嵌入方案 [6]。

在针对位置嵌入的消融实验中，结果显示不同编码位置信息的方式之间几乎没有性能差异，这表明在补丁级输入下，不同的空间信息编码策略对学习空间关系的难度相当 [2]。此外，文档中详细列出了尝试过的多种位置嵌入变体（如添加至输入端、每层开头添加等），涵盖了 1 维和 2 维的情况，但并未提及“卷积位置嵌入”这一特定方法 [10]。因此，基于现有资料，ViT 的默认配置并非卷积位置嵌入。
judge_reason: 检索到的上下文明确显示ViT使用标准一维位置嵌入且未提及卷积位置嵌入，直接支持回答关键事实；回答内容完全基于检索到的上下文，无额外补全。

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::19
retrieved_contexts.summary: Inductive bias. We note that Vision Transformer has much less image-specific inductive bias than CNNs. In CNNs, locality, two-dimensional neighborhood structure, and translation equivariance are baked into each layer throughout the whole model. In ViT, only MLP layers are local and translationally equivariant, while the self-attention layers are global. The two-dimensional neighborhood structure is used very sparingly: in the beginning of the model by cutting the image into patches and at fine-tuning time for adjusting the position embeddings for images of different resolution (as described below). Other than that, the position embeddings at initialization time carry no information about the 2D positions of the patches and all spatial relations between the patches have to be learned from scratch.

As we can see, while there is a large gap between the performances of the model with no positional embedding and models with positional embedding, there is little to no difference between different ways of encoding positional information. We speculate that since our Transformer encoder operates on patch-level inputs, as opposed to pixel-level, the differences in how to encode spatial information is less important. More precisely, in patch-level inputs, the spatial dimensions are much smaller than the original pixel-level inputs, e.g., $1 4 \times 1 4$ as opposed to $2 2 4 \times 2 2 4$ , and learning to represent the spatial relations in this resolution is equally easy for these different positional encoding strategies. Even so, the specific pattern of position embedding similarity learned by the network depends on the training hyperparameters (Figure 10).

retrieved_contexts.summary: We evaluate the representation learning capabilities of ResNet, Vision Transformer (ViT), and the hybrid. To understand the data requirements of each model, we pre-train on datasets of varying size and evaluate many benchmark tasks. When considering the computational cost of pre-training the model, ViT performs very favourably, attaining state of the art on most recognition benchmarks at a lower pre-training cost. Lastly, we perform a small experiment using self-supervision, and show that self-supervised ViT holds promise for the future.

retrieved_contexts.summary: However, the picture changes if the models are trained on larger datasets (14M-300M images). We find that large scale training trumps inductive bias. Our Vision Transformer (ViT) attains excellent results when pre-trained at sufficient scale and transferred to tasks with fewer datapoints. When pre-trained on the public ImageNet-21k dataset or the in-house JFT-300M dataset, ViT approaches or beats state of the art on multiple image recognition benchmarks. In particular, the best model reaches the accuracy of $8 8 . 5 5 \%$ on ImageNet, $9 0 . 7 2 \%$ on ImageNet-ReaL, $9 4 . 5 5 \%$ on CIFAR-100, and $7 7 . 6 3 \%$ on the VTAB suite of 19 tasks.

Position embeddings are added to the patch embeddings to retain positional information. We use standard learnable 1D position embeddings, since we have not observed significant performance gains from using more advanced 2D-aware position embeddings (Appendix D.4). The resulting sequence of embedding vectors serves as input to the encoder.

The Transformer encoder (Vaswani et al., 2017) consists of alternating layers of multiheaded selfattention (MSA, see Appendix A) and MLP blocks (Eq. 2, 3). Layernorm (LN) is applied before every block, and residual connections after every block (Wang et al., 2019; Baevski & Auli, 2019).

The MLP contains two layers with a GELU non-linearity.

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::100
retrieved_contexts.summary: Axial Attention (Huang et al., 2020; Ho et al., 2019) is a simple, yet effective technique to run selfattention on large inputs that are organized as multidimensional tensors. The general idea of axial attention is to perform multiple attention operations, each along a single axis of the input tensor, instead of applying 1-dimensional attention to the flattened version of the input. In axial attention, each attention mixes information along a particular axis, while keeping information along the other axes independent. Along this line, Wang et al. (2020b) proposed the AxialResNet model in which all the convolutions with kernel size $3 \times 3$ in a ResNet50 are replaced by axial self-attention, i.e. a row and column attention, augmented by relative positional encoding. We have implemented AxialResNet as a baseline model.3.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::5
retrieved_contexts.summary:
Attention mechanisms have become an integral part of compelling sequence modeling and transduction models in various tasks, allowing modeling of dependencies without regard to their distance in the input or output sequences [2, 19]. In all but a few cases [27], however, such attention mechanisms are used in conjunction with a recurrent network.

In this work we propose the Transformer, a model architecture eschewing recurrence and instead relying entirely on an attention mechanism to draw global dependencies between input and output. The Transformer allows for significantly more parallelization and can reach a new state of the art in translation quality after being trained for as little as twelve hours on eight P100 GPUs.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::7
retrieved_contexts.summary:
Self-attention, sometimes called intra-attention is an attention mechanism relating different positions of a single sequence in order to compute a representation of the sequence. Self-attention has been used successfully in a variety of tasks including reading comprehension, abstractive summarization, textual entailment and learning task-independent sentence representations [4, 27, 28, 22].

End-to-end memory networks are based on a recurrent attention mechanism instead of sequencealigned recurrence and have been shown to perform well on simple-language question answering and language modeling tasks [34].
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::32
retrieved_contexts.summary: As side benefit, self-attention could yield more interpretable models. We inspect attention distributions from our models and present and discuss examples in the appendix. Not only do individual attention heads clearly learn to perform different tasks, many appear to exhibit behavior related to the syntactic and semantic structure of the sentences.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf

Many of the attention heads attend to a distant dependency of the verb ‘making’, completing the phrase ‘making...more difficult’. Attentions here shown only for the word ‘making’. retrieved_contexts.summary: To better encode edge features into attention layers, we propose a new edge encoding method in Graphormer. The attention mechanism needs to estimate correlations for each node pair $( v _ { i } , v _ { j } )$ , and we believe the edges connecting them should be considered in the correlation as in [34, 51]. For each ordered node pair $( v _ { i } , v _ { j } )$ , we find (one of) the shortest path ${ \bf S P } _ { i j } = ( e _ { 1 } , e _ { 2 } , . . . , e _ { N } )$ from $v _ { i }$ to $v _ { j }$ , and compute an average of the dot-products of the edge feature and a learnable embedding along the path. The proposed edge encoding incorporates edge features via a bias term to the attention module. Concretely, we modify the $( i , j )$ -element of $A$ in Eq. (3) further with the edge encoding $c _ { i j }$ as:

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::89
retrieved_contexts.summary: Complexity. Similar to regular Transformer, the attention mechanism in Graphormer scales quadratically with the number of nodes $n$ in the input graph, which may be prohibitively expensive for large $n$ and precludes its usage in settings with limited computational resources. Recently, many solutions have been proposed to address this problem in Transformer [25, 52, 57, 37]. This issue would be greatly benefit from the future development of efficient Graphormer.

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::14
retrieved_contexts.summary:
We call our particular attention "Scaled Dot-Product Attention" (Figure 2). The input consists of queries and keys of dimension $d _ { k }$ , and values of dimension √ $d _ { v }$ . We compute the dot products of the query with all keys, divide each by $\sqrt { d _ { k } }$ , and apply a softmax function to obtain the weights on the values.

In practice, we compute the attention function on a set of queries simultaneously, packed together into a matrix $Q$ . The keys and values are also packed together into matrices $K$ and $V$ . We compute the matrix of outputs as:

$$
\mathrm { A t t e n t i o n } ( Q , K , V ) = \mathrm { s o f t m a x } ( \frac { Q K ^ { T } } { \sqrt { d _ { k } } } ) V
$$
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::53
retrieved_contexts.summary:
In this work, we presented the Transformer, the first sequence transduction model based entirely on attention, replacing the recurrent layers most commonly used in encoder-decoder architectures with multi-headed self-attention.

For translation tasks, the Transformer can be trained significantly faster than architectures based on recurrent or convolutional layers. On both WMT 2014 English-to-German and WMT 2014 English-to-French translation tasks, we achieve a new state of the art. In the former task our best model outperforms even all previously reported ensembles.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::2
retrieved_contexts.summary: The Transformer [49] is well acknowledged as the most powerful neural network in modelling sequential data, such as natural language [11, 35, 6] and speech [17]. Model variants built upon Transformer have also been shown great performance in computer vision [12, 36] and programming language [19, 63, 44]. However, to the best of our knowledge, Transformer has still not been the de-facto standard on public graph representation leaderboards [22, 14, 21]. There are many attempts of leveraging Transformer into the graph domain, but the only effective way is replacing some key modules (e.g., feature aggregation) in classic GNN variants by the softmax attention [50, 7, 23, 51, 61, 46, 13]. Therefore, it is still an open question whether Transformer architecture is suitable to model graphs and how to make it work in graph representation learning.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Vaswani 等 - 2023 - Attention Is All You Need.pdf::chunk::8
retrieved_contexts.summary: To the best of our knowledge, however, the Transformer is the first transduction model relying entirely on self-attention to compute representations of its input and output without using sequencealigned RNNs or convolution. In the following sections, we will describe the Transformer, motivate self-attention and discuss its advantages over models such as [17, 18] and [9].

question: 在 Vision Transformer (ViT) (Dosovitskiy et al., 2021) 中，作者提出了“多尺度 ViT”（Multi-Scale ViT）变体，通过在不同层级使用不同尺寸的 patch 来捕捉多尺度特征，从而在 ImageNet 上取得了新的 SOTA。请问这是 ViT 论文中的正式模型变体吗？
ground_truth: 文献未提及。
answer:
根据检索到的本地知识库内容，文中未明确提及名为“多尺度 ViT"（Multi-Scale ViT）的正式模型变体，文献未提及该变体。

提供的文档中定义的 Vision Transformer 模型变体主要基于 BERT 配置，包括“Base”、“Large”和“Huge”三种规模 [1]。这些变体在命名上直接关联固定的输入 patch 尺寸，例如“ViT-L/16”表示“Large”变体配合 $16 \times 16$ 的输入 patch 大小，并未描述在不同层级使用不同尺寸 patch 的架构设计 [1]。此外，文档中关于 patch 尺寸的讨论主要集中在全局缩放实验上，指出减小 patch 尺寸会增加有效序列长度并带来性能提升，但这被视为一种超参数调整而非网络内部的多尺度结构变体 [3]。表 1 详细列出的模型变体参数中，也仅包含层数、隐藏层大小、MLP 大小等固定配置，未涉及多尺度 patch 设置 [6]。因此，基于现有检索内容，无法确认该变体为本文正式提出的模型。
judge_reason: 检索到的上下文明确列出了ViT的正式模型变体（Base、Large、Huge）并讨论了patch尺寸，直接支持了文献未提及多尺度ViT的事实；答案严格依据上下文内容，无常识补全，忠实反映了检索信息。

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::26
retrieved_contexts.summary: Model Variants. We base ViT configurations on those used for BERT (Devlin et al., 2019), as summarized in Table 1. The “Base” and “Large” models are directly adopted from BERT and we add the larger “Huge” model. In what follows we use brief notation to indicate the model size and the input patch size: for instance, ViT-L/16 means the “Large” variant with $1 6 \times 1 6$ input patch size. Note that the Transformer’s sequence length is inversely proportional to the square of the patch size, thus models with smaller patch size are computationally more expensive.

retrieved_contexts.summary: Moreover, we have modified ViT to process inputs in the 2-dimensional shape, instead of a 1- dimensional sequence of patches, and incorporate Axial Transformer blocks, in which instead of a self-attention followed by an MLP, we have a a row-self-attention plus an MLP followed by a column-self-attention plus an MLP.

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::87
retrieved_contexts.summary: We ran ablations on scaling different dimensions of the Transformer architecture to find out which are best suited for scaling to very large models. Figure 8 shows 5-shot performance on ImageNet for different configurations. All configurations are based on a ViT model with 8 layers, $D = 1 0 2 4$ , $D _ { M L P } = 2 0 4 8$ and a patch size of 32, the intersection of all lines. We can see that scaling the depth results in the biggest improvements which are clearly visible up until 64 layers. However, diminishing returns are already visible after 16 layers. Interestingly, scaling the width of the network seems to result in the smallest changes. Decreasing the patch size and thus increasing the effective sequence length shows surprisingly robust improvements without introducing parameters. These findings suggest that compute might be a better predictor of performance than the number of parameters, and that scaling should emphasize depth over width if any. Overall, we find that scaling all dimensions proportionally results in robust improvements.

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf::chunk::19
retrieved_contexts.summary: Inductive bias. We note that Vision Transformer has much less image-specific inductive bias than CNNs. In CNNs, locality, two-dimensional neighborhood structure, and translation equivariance are baked into each layer throughout the whole model. In ViT, only MLP layers are local and translationally equivariant, while the self-attention layers are global. The two-dimensional neighborhood structure is used very sparingly: in the beginning of the model by cutting the image into patches and at fine-tuning time for adjusting the position embeddings for images of different resolution (as described below). Other than that, the position embeddings at initialization time carry no information about the 2D positions of the patches and all spatial relations between the patches have to be learned from scratch.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Dosovitskiy 等 - 2021 - An Image is Worth 16x16 Words Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

The first layer of the Vision Transformer linearly projects the flattened patches into a lower-dimensional space (Eq. 1). The components resemble plausible basis functions for a low-dimensional representation of the fine structure within each patch.

To understand the data requirements of each model, we pre-train on datasets of varying size and evaluate many benchmark tasks. When considering the computational cost of pre-training the model, ViT performs very favourably, attaining state of the art on most recognition benchmarks at a lower pre-training cost. Lastly, we perform a small experiment using self-supervision, and show that self-supervised ViT holds promise for the future.

question: Graphormer (Ying et al., 2021) 中，作者提出使用“图同构网络”（Graph Isomorphism Network, GIN）作为其空间编码的替代方案，并证明 GIN 与基于最短路径距离的编码在表达能力上是等价的。请问这是 Graphormer 的理论贡献之一吗？
ground_truth: 文献未提及。
answer:
根据检索到的本地知识库内容，该说法并未得到支持。文档明确指出 Graphormer 的空间编码（Spatial Encoding）是基于节点间的最短路径距离（Shortest Path Distance, SPD）构建的，而非图同构网络（GIN）[2][4]。具体而言，作者定义空间关系函数 $\phi(v_i, v_j)$ 为连接节点间的最短路径距离，并将其作为偏置项加入自注意力模块 [4]。

关于 GIN，文档在理论分析部分证明，通过选择合适的权重和距离函数，Graphormer 层可以表示包括 GIN 在内的流行 GNN 模型的聚合与组合步骤，表明 Graphormer 具有更强的表达能力并能涵盖 GIN 等模型 [7]。然而，文中并未提及将 GIN 作为空间编码的替代方案，也未证明 GIN 与基于最短路径距离的编码在表达能力上是等价的 [7]。文档列出的理论贡献主要包括证明 Graphormer 能超越经典消息传递 GNN 的表达能力（如 1-WL 测试限制），以及展示其能代表多种 GNN 变体 [3][7]。

综上所述，基于现有检索内容，无法确认“使用 GIN 作为空间编码替代方案并证明其与最短路径距离编码等价”是 Graphormer 的理论贡献；相反，文档明确说明其空间编码采用最短路径距离，且 Graphormer 能代表 GIN 模型 [2][4][7]。
judge_reason: 检索到的上下文显示Graphormer空间编码基于最短路径距离，且能表示GIN模型，但未提及GIN作为替代方案或证明其与SPD编码等价。

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::13
retrieved_contexts.summary: In this section, we present our Graphormer for graph tasks. First, we elaborate on several key designs in the Graphormer, which serve as an inductive bias in the neural network to learn the graph representation. We further provide the detailed implementations of Graphormer. Finally, we show that our proposed Graphormer is more powerful since popular GNN models [26, 54, 18] are its special cases.
retrieved_contexts.doc_title: Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf

retrieved_contexts.note_id: doc_Ying 等 - 2021 - Do Transformers Really Perform Bad for Graph Representation.pdf::chunk::5
retrieved_contexts.summary: Second, we propose a novel Spatial Encoding in Graphormer to capture the structural relation between nodes. One notable geometrical property that distinguishes graph-structured data from other structured data, e.g., language, images, is that there does not exist a canonical grid to embed the graph. In fact, nodes can only lie in a non-Euclidean space and are linked by edges. To model such structural information, for each node pair, we assign a learnable embedding based on their spatial relation. Multiple measurements in the literature could be leveraged for modeling spatial relations. For a general purpose, we use the distance of the shortest path between any two nodes as a demonstration, which will be encoded as a bias term in the softmax attention and help the model accurately capture the spatial dependency in a graph. In addition, sometimes there is additional spatial information contained in edge features, such as the type of bond between two atoms in a molecular graph. We design a new edge encoding method to further take such signal into the Transformer layers. To be concrete, for each node pair, we compute an average of dot-products of the edge features and learnable embeddings along the shortest path, then use it in the attention module. Equipped with these encodings, Graphormer could better model the relationship for node pairs and represent the graph.

retrieved_contexts.summary: However, for graphs, nodes are not arranged as a sequence. They can lie in a multi-dimensional spatial space and are linked by edges. To encode the structural information of a graph in the model, we propose a novel Spatial Encoding. Concretely, for any graph $G$ , we consider a function $\phi \left( v _ { i } , v _ { j } \right) : V \times V \to \mathbb { R }$ which measures the spatial relation between $v _ { i }$ and $v _ { j }$ in graph $G$ . The function $\phi$ can be defined by the connectivity between the nodes in the graph. In this paper, we choose $\phi ( v _ { i } , v _ { j } )$ to be the distance of the shortest path (SPD) between $v _ { i }$ and $v _ { j }$ if the two nodes are connected. If not, we set the output of $\phi$ to be a special value, i.e., -1. We assign each (feasible) output value a learnable scalar which will serve as a bias term in the self-attention module. Denote $A _ { i j }$ as the $( i , j )$ -element of the Query-Key product matrix $A$ , we have:
